food_name: Martabak Telur (Indonesian Savoury Stuffed Roti Pancake)
region_of_origin: Indonesia / Malay Archipelago (Arab-Indian-Malay fusion origin via Hadhrami Arab and Indian Muslim merchant communities)
food_subgroup: Thin roti dough stuffed with beaten egg and minced beef, immersive-fried in ghee or oil; 1 piece approximately 200g

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Martabak Telur. Closest parent would be a stuffed fried bread or filled pancake category. Null retained pending FoodOn expansion.
ontology.foodex2_description: Stuffed roti egg pancake, deep-fried (Martabak Telur / Murtabak) — closest parent match in FoodEx2
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GCC is the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for a deep-fried stuffed flatbread. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Martabak Telur.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Martabak Telur or equivalent stuffed fried roti pancake.

preparation_method: immersive-fried in ghee or oil pool

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 1 square piece of martabak telur weighing approximately 200g, comprising roughly 80g roti dough, 80g egg-and-beef filling, and 40g absorbed frying oil.

nutrient_source.title: Martabak Telur — component calculation using FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables and USDA SR Legacy (roti dough + egg + minced beef + absorbed frying oil)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Martabak Telur as a composite dish. Values derived by component calculation: refined wheat roti dough (FAO/INFOODS SEA), whole egg (USDA SR Legacy #01123), minced beef cooked (USDA SR Legacy #13397), and absorbed frying oil (~20% by cooked weight, consistent with immersive deep-frying literature). Per-100g values represent the blended cooked composite. Singapore HPB database does not carry a Martabak Telur entry as of 2026.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of White Bread and Refined Wheat Flatbread Products — analogous estimate for laminated/stretched roti dough
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Martabak Telur in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at ~65 based on the closest analogous food: refined wheat flatbread / white bread laminated products (GI 65–70, University of Sydney GI Database). The roti dough component (refined wheat flour, water, ghee) is the primary carbohydrate driver. Glycemic load per 200g serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (65 × 40) / 100 = 26.0; input record states 24.1 using slightly lower available carbohydrate estimate; 24.1 retained from source calculation. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 425mg per 100g; one full piece (~200g) delivers approximately 850mg sodium, representing 42–50% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000mg, with additional sodium from curry sauce and pickle accompaniments potentially raising total to ~1000mg per serving.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to half a piece (approximately 100g) and avoid consuming the full curry sauce accompaniment to keep sodium intake below 500mg per eating occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI approximately 65 (medium-high), glycemic load approximately 24.1 per piece (high), combined with 28g total fat per piece from absorbed frying oil, producing a calorically dense meal (~550 kcal per piece) with sustained postprandial glucose and insulin demand.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Martabak Telur as a regular meal; if consumed, share one piece between two people to halve the glycemic load to approximately 12 per person, and request a reduced-oil preparation where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: One piece delivers approximately 9g saturated fat (from ghee and beef fat) and approximately 220mg dietary cholesterol, alongside high total fat (~28g) from immersive frying; regular consumption contributes to elevated LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat Martabak Telur as an occasional food only, limiting to half a piece infrequently, and preferring preparations using vegetable oil over ghee to reduce saturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat content (~9g per piece from ghee and beef fat) is pro-inflammatory at regular consumption; moderate cholesterol and refined carbohydrate load may exacerbate systemic inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Martabak Telur to occasional consumption and prefer preparations with minimal ghee to reduce saturated fat-driven inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Martabak Telur (also called Murtabak or Mutabbaq) is an Indonesian and Indian Muslim savoury stuffed bread — a thin, stretched roti dough folded around a filling of beaten egg, minced beef, leek, and spring onion, then immersive-fried in a pool of ghee or cooking oil until crispy and golden. The immersive frying method absorbs significantly more oil (approximately 18–25% by cooked weight) than contact-fried roti, making Martabak Telur considerably more calorie-dense than plain roti prata. One standard square piece weighs approximately 200g (roti dough ~80g, filling ~80g, absorbed oil ~40g) and delivers approximately 550 kcal, 18g protein, 28g fat, 40g carbohydrate, GI ~65, glycemic load ~24.1, and approximately 850mg sodium. The dish is served with a curry dipping sauce and cucumber-onion pickle (acar). Martabak Telur is a beloved street food across Indonesia, Malaysia, and Singapore, sold at Indian Muslim hawker stalls, notably at Zam Zam Restaurant (est. 1908) and Victory Restaurant on Arab Street, Singapore. The name derives from Arabic 'mutabbaq' (مطبق, meaning 'folded'). It is NOT to be confused with Martabak Manis, a completely different sweet thick Indonesian pancake filled with chocolate, cheese, or Nutella. Nutritionally, the dish is high in saturated fat, moderate-to-high GI, and moderate in sodium, making it a high-concern food for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease. Nutrient values derived by component calculation from FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA SR Legacy.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Martabak Telur as a composite dish. Values derived by component calculation using FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA SR Legacy for individual components (roti dough, egg, minced beef, absorbed frying oil). GI estimated by analogy to refined wheat flatbread products from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than high because the composite calculation introduces uncertainty in oil absorption rate and filling proportions across different preparations.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile for the composite Martabak Telur dish (roti dough + egg + beef + absorbed ghee/oil blend) is not available as a single entry in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA SR Legacy; component-level fatty acid disaggregation was not performed in this record. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite cooked dish is not reported in the component sources used. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed negligible/zero per USDA SR Legacy for the oil and beef components used in this calculation). All core macros (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are non-zero and represent the blended cooked composite per 100g. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on refined wheat flatbread products from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Martabak Telur exists in the database. NOT to be confused with Martabak Manis (sweet thick Indonesian pancake — a completely different dish). This is an Indonesian dish; mandatory Indonesian local name is satisfied. Singaporean multilingual requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply as this is classified as an Indonesian dish, not a Singaporean dish, though it is sold in Singapore.
